public long calculateSum(int val, int length) {

   // calculates the minimal sum for a sequence of 'length' elements, where the last element (rightmost) is val, and each previous element is at most 1 less than the next.

   // which is same as the left or right part.

   // For example, for the left part, the sequence is built from the index backwards.

   // So the sum is same as if we have a sequence that starts at some value and decreases by 1 each step, but not below 1.

   // So same logic as before.

   int m = length;

   long sum = 0;

   if (val >= m) {

       // the sum is (val + (val - m + 1)) * m / 2

       long firstTerm = val;

       long lastTerm = val - m + 1;

       sum = (firstTerm + lastTerm) * m / 2;

   } else {

       // sum is sum from val down to 1 (if possible) plus (m - val) * 1

       long sumDecreasing = (long) val * (val + 1) / 2;

       sum = sumDecreasing + (m - val) * 1L;

   }

   return sum;

}

public int maxValue(int n, int index, int maxSum) {

    int low = 1, high = maxSum;

    int ans = 0;

    while (low <= high) {

        int mid = (low + high) / 2;

        long sum = getSum(mid, index + 1, n - index, n);

        if (sum <= maxSum) {

            ans = mid;

            low = mid + 1;

        } else {

            high = mid - 1;

        }

    }

    return ans;

}

public int maxValue(int n, int index, int maxSum) {
    int low = 1;
    int high = maxSum;
    int ans = 1;
    while (low <= high) {
        int mid = (low + high) / 2;
        long val = mid;
        long leftLen = (long) index + 1;
        long rightLen = (long) (n - index);
        long leftSum, rightSum;
        
        // Calculate leftSum
        if (val >= leftLen) {
            long first = val;
            long last = val - leftLen + 1;
            leftSum = (first + last) * leftLen / 2;
        } else {
            long sumDecrease = val * (val + 1) / 2;
            long countOnes = leftLen - val;
            leftSum = sumDecrease + countOnes;
        }
        
        // Calculate rightSum
        if (val >= rightLen) {
            long first = val;
            long last = val - rightLen + 1;
            rightSum = (first + last) * rightLen / 2;
        } else {
            long sumDecrease = val * (val + 1) / 2;
            long countOnes = rightLen - val;
            rightSum = sumDecrease + countOnes;
        }
        
        long total = leftSum + rightSum - val;
        if (total <= maxSum) {
            ans = mid;
            low = mid + 1;
        } else {
            high = mid - 1;
        }
    }
    return ans;
}

solution: ```java
class Solution {
    public int maxValue(int n, int index, int maxSum) {
        int low = 1;
        int high = maxSum;
        int ans = 1;
        while (low <= high) {
            int mid = (low + high) / 2;
            long val = mid;
            long leftLen = (long) index + 1;
            long rightLen = (long) (n - index);
            long leftSum, rightSum;

            if (val >= leftLen) {
                long first = val;
                long last = val - leftLen + 1;
                leftSum = (first + last) * leftLen / 2;
            } else {
                long sumDecrease = val * (val + 1) / 2;
                long countOnes = leftLen - val;
                leftSum = sumDecrease + countOnes;
            }

            if (val >= rightLen) {
                long first = val;
                long last = val - rightLen + 1;
                rightSum = (first + last) * rightLen / 2;
            } else {
                long sumDecrease = val * (val + 1) / 2;
                long countOnes = rightLen - val;
                rightSum = sumDecrease + countOnes;
            }

            long total = leftSum + rightSum - val;
            if (total <= maxSum) {
                ans = mid;
                low = mid + 1;
            } else {
                high = mid - 1;
            }
        }
        return ans;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log(maxSum))
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Uses binary search to find the maximum value at the given index. For each candidate value, calculates the minimal array sum required using arithmetic series formulas. Efficiently handles large constraints with logarithmic time complexity.
